name: sherline_6axis
description: MKII Scanner: Sherline CNC mill (X/Y/Z) + 4th-axis specimen rotary (A, about X) + quill-replacing camera tilt rotary (B, about Y) carrying a focus rail (R).

scene_geometry.coordinate_system: Z-up, right-handed; origin at specimen (chuck) center
scene_geometry.description: Camera is mounted on the focus rail (R), which is mounted on the camera-tilt rotary (B) that replaced the mill quill on the Z column. At B=0 the camera looks straight DOWN (-Z) at the specimen. Increasing B rotates the optical axis about the Y axis (in the XZ plane), lowering camera elevation toward horizontal at B=90. The specimen is held in the A rotary table (rotation axis parallel to X) on the X/Y bed. X/Y/Z reposition to keep the specimen framed and at the correct focus distance as B and R change.

specimen.notes: Specimen clamped in the 4th-axis rotary chuck; held at the origin via coordinated X/Y/Z bed/quill motion.

X.notes: Mill table X. Frames specimen and compensates camera tilt offset.

Z.notes: Mill quill/column Z. Carries the B tilt rotary and camera; coarse camera height + focus-distance compensation.

R.positive_direction: Toward specimen along the optical axis (-Z at B=0). Direction rotates with B.
R.notes: Fine focus rail on the B rotary. Sweeps focus for focus stacking. Its travel direction is rigidly tied to B.

A.notes: 4th-axis rotary table. Specimen rotates about the X axis (horizontal).

B.pivot_point: on the Z column where the quill was (moves with Z); see ik_geometry.b_pivot
B.zero_convention: B=0 -> camera optical axis points -Z (straight down)
B.positive_direction: Positive B tilts the optical axis toward +X (in the XZ plane); B=90 -> horizontal
B.notes: Rotary table replacing the quill. Tilts the whole camera+rail assembly about Y.

camera.notes: Optical axis is -Z at B=0. Orientation set by B; position set by Z, B, and R.

framing_compensation.description: When B (tilt) or R (focus) change, the camera's entrance pupil moves along an arc about the B pivot. X/Y/Z adjust to keep the specimen centered in frame at the target focus distance.
framing_compensation.notes: Exact compensation depends on ik_geometry (pivot location + rail offsets), which must be measured/calibrated. Implemented in kinematics.py (planned).

ik_geometry._comment: Quantitative geometry the inverse kinematics needs. Fill these in by measurement/calibration before IK output is trustworthy. Frames are in mm, B-frame = the tilt rotary's local frame at the given B.
ik_geometry.notes: b_pivot_at_home = tilt-axis pivot in machine coords at Z home. b_pivot_to_camera_at_R0_in_Bframe = vector from pivot to camera entrance pupil at R=0, expressed in the B-frame. focus_distance_per_R_mm = how working distance changes per mm of R travel (typically 1.0 if R moves the camera directly along the optical axis).

tilt_settings._comment: B=0 is top-down; B=90 is horizontal/side view.

pose_calculation.notes: Camera tilts about Y via the B axis (B=0 = straight down). For each shot: 1) choose specimen orientation A and camera tilt B, 2) place camera on the optical axis at the target focus distance (set by R), 3) compensate X/Y/Z so the specimen stays centered, 4) sweep R for the focus stack. Replaces the obsolete camera-orbit-about-Z model from v2.0.
